exercises.description: Order the sentences (B-G) below to make a story. {v} The first sentence of the story (A) is given for you as an example. {v} A. - The Chinese Empire formed the largest civilisation in history.
exercises.label: Reading Part 2

questions.title: B The Han dynasty ruled from 206 BC to 220 AD, this period is known as the golden age of China, a long period of stability and prosperity.
C The last dynasty was the Qing Dynasty, after which The Republic of China came to power in 1911 and ruled till 1949.
D The Tang dynasty ruled from 618 to 907 AD, this dynasty is considered the highest point of the empire, both politically and culturally.
E The most influential rulers of China were the Han and the Tang dynasties.
F The Empire started in 1700 BC.
G 4,000 years of Imperial rule finished with the formation of the republic.

exercises.description: The sentences below are from some instructions, Put these sentences in the right order. The first sentences are done for you {v} A. If you are going to the airport, please follow these instructions.
exercises.label: Reading Part 2

questions.title: B Pass through the security and go to your gate
C Check your boarding and go to your respective gate and enter the plane.
D Next you have to arrive three hours early to the flight.
E Upon your arrival go to the check-in counters of your airline, check-in your luggage and receive your boarding pass.
F You should pack your bags carefully and be aware of their weight and size.

exercises.heading: Sports
exercises.description: Person A {v} Having my children take part in group sports like football and basketball was always a priority of mine, as I found it had a strong, positive impact on myself while growing up. While it is true that a certain level of stress may come as a result of involvement in sports, due to training and matches at the weekend, the benefits definitely outweigh the negatives. I strongly promote the involvement of sports in our lives. {v} Person B {v} I think that water sports, both in the sea and in the pool, are probably one of the most difficult types. To surf, for example, you have to be physically fit because it can be extremely tiring. Swimming is another great, full-body workout that should not be underestimated. While all water sports require extreme focus, discipline and good instructors, diving is especially intense as it not only requires physical fitness but great technique. {v} Person C {v} While I would not call myself an athlete of any sort, if I had to choose a sport, I would go with some type of sport you can play by the water that allows me to participate while wearing my bathing suit. Sports are supposed to be enjoyable, and turning it into some type of competition takes away that fun. I just don’t understand the appeal of putting on a track suit and running laps around a field to win some type of prize. {v} Person D {v} The day I had to put down my racket was probably one of the saddest days of my life. Being a tennis player had taught me so much about myself and the physical limits I could push myself to. My coaches were vital in my success and I can’t thank them enough for teaching me the skills I used to win that championship. I always wonder how my experience could have changed if I hadn’t competed alone.
exercises.subtitle: Four people comment on an article about the positives and negatives of sports. Read their comments and answer the questions below. {v}
exercises.label: Reading Part 4

questions.title: 1. Who thinks that water sports are particularly hard?
2. Who thinks that their success was because of their trainers?
3. Who prefers to relax than play a sport?
4. Who thinks that everyone should participate in sports?
5. Who thinks that team sports are especially beneficial?
6. Who was upset about leaving a sport?
7. Who thinks that strict training is necessary in a certain sport?

exercises.description: Read the text below. Match the headings A-H to the paragraphs 19-25.
exercises.label: Reading Part 5

answers: E. Ageing - An account of growing old.
B. How to change your genetic disposition
questions.title: 19. The question about what “ageing” is can be answered simply by saying that it is the process of {v} growing old. It refers to the point in life when individual cells stop dividing. As you grow in {v} years, your wisdom and knowledge may increase, but you also begin to see decline in {v} psychological and physical attributes, as the brain and the body slow in pace. For the elderly, {v} reacting late, moving slowly, and misunderstanding what people say to them is quite common. {v} As old age makes the body weak, it becomes more prone to diseases, and research {v} indicates that at least two-thirds of deaths every year are direct results of ageing.

20. Lifestyle plays a significant role in the speed of one’s ageing process. A controlled diet may slow {v} the aging process, and may help to maintain overall wellness. Fruits, vegetables, and whole {v} grains are beneficial for enhancing your life and overall wellness. On the contrary, an {v} undisciplined lifestyle may cost you precious years. Excessive fat, salt, and sugar consumption {v} may shave years off your life, as these are directly connected to health issues such as heart {v} disease, diabetes, and other life-threatening illnesses.

21.Though heart disease may be caused, in part, by one’s dietary habits, it’s also contributed to {v} by the work of 100 different genes, something outside of your control. Despite there being no {v} solution to the genetic factors, there are options to help protect yourself against their effects, {v} as was previously mentioned. Longevity is a hereditary aspect, so having long-living relatives {v} can point to higher chances of your own longevity. There is a common belief that genes play a {v} large role in how quickly one grows. Besides having the right genes, other factors can {v} increase your life expectancy as well.

answers: F. Other lifestyle choices that influence overall health
questions.title: 22. Despite these findings, however, there’s no question as to whether or not premature ageing and {v} disease can be prevented by lifestyle choices; the answer is an emphatic yes. Other means {v} of protection include giving up smoking, and engaging in some amount of regular physical {v} activity. Smoking is a dangerous habit, as it can damage your lungs and respiratory system, and {v} lead to various types of cancer. If you wish to delay age-related death, you must prioritize {v} regular exercise; there is no alternative. Keeping an active mind by, for example, solving {v} puzzles or trying to figure out new and unfamiliar tasks, like how to use Facebook or Twitter, is {v} also recommended in maintaining one’s overall health.

answers: D. The effects seen in Europe regarding longevity
questions.title: 23. Europeans are now living longer than they used to even a decade ago. Through thorough {v} research into the causes of deaths, the governments of various European nations have been {v} able to come up with solutions to prevent death from age-related disease. As of now, even if {v} people are suffering from a certain disease, it’s become less likely that they die from it. The {v} average life expectancy of a European male is 79 years, while that of a female is 84 years.

answers: G. Factors contributing to improvements in Europe’s health
questions.title: 24. Some of the reasons behind the rise in life expectancy in Europe include vaccination, health {v} sanitation, and public health measures. Apart from these factors, one’s socio-economic status is {v} indisputably one of the most vital influences on the wellbeing and health of an individual. {v} Even though life expectancy is on the rise in different parts of Europe, there is a stark contrast {v} between the average age of death of the well-off and those who struggle financially.

answers: H. What role does social status play on one’s life expectancy?
questions.title: 25. It has been proven that, in developed countries, the rich tend to have healthier eating and {v} drinking habits than the average citizen. Meanwhile, those with incomes at or below the poverty {v} line have been shown less able to change their eating habits for the better. An individual with {v} a better income has access to more choices regarding diet, and having these options at their {v} disposal allows them to select healthier options, regardless of the cost. On the other hand, {v} for an individual living on the streets, without stable living conditions or a fixed income, smoking {v} a cigarette or eating fast food, though costly to both their health and budget, may be the only {v} thing that can get them through the day.

exercises.title: 15. Listen to a woman ask her friend for advice on getting a pet for her kids. Read the opinions below and decide whose opinion matches the statements made by the man, the woman or both the man and the woman. {v} Who expresses which opinion? {x}

questions.title: Question 1: What is her opinion of the rules behind low-cost tickets? {x}
answers: She believes that airlines provide fantastic low-cost tickets for their clients.
She thinks that it is difficult to get low-cost tickets that work for everyone.
She thinks it is unfair that clients must always purchase two-way tickets to get a low-cost ticket.

questions.title: Question 2: What is her overall opinion towards low-cost tickets? {x}
answers: There are drawbacks, but ultimately it is possible to save a lot of money.
The hidden fees make the price of a low-cost ticket the same as any other ticket at regular price.
Purchasing low-cost tickets can be problematic if you change the dates of your holiday.

exercises.description: This is the Aptis Listening Test.

exercises.title: 16. Listen to a travel agent talk about how to get the best deals on flights and answer the questions below. {x}

questions.title: Question 1: What are the dieticians primary thoughts about veganism? {x}
answers: It is a great idea for everyone because it can minimize the risk of diseases like cancer and diabetes due to less consumption of animal fat.
Following a vegan diet means that you are most likely to live longer than the average human being.
The human body is in need of certain nutrients that a vegan diet may not be able to provide which may cause certain deficiencies in the body.

questions.title: Question 2: What does he think must be considered before changing to a vegan diet? {x}
answers: It is important to understand what you can and cannot eat when following a vegan diet.
The only people who should follow a vegan diet are those that are suffering from certain diseases.
There are certain nutrients you must find other ways to provide your body with while following a vegan diet.

title: Kitchen Chat
description: Practice quick replies, real conversations, and everyday English through the lens of a cooking club — from recipes to gossip.
